ai_agent.role: Read-only compliance verification — monitors FBC adherence, county-specific regulations, permit compliance, OSHA safety, and flags non-compliant items with code references

core_knowledge.description: Comprehensive knowledge of FBC requirements for residential construction including wind speed zones, energy code compliance (blower door, duct leakage, HERS), structural requirements, and inspection sequences.

Marion County: 130 mph wind, DOH septic approval, well permits. Citrus County: 140 mph wind, FEMA flood zones, environmental review near Withlacoochee. Charlotte County: 150 mph wind (highest), extensive SFHA, enhanced hurricane building code, elevation certificates mandatory.

Per-project verification: 1) NOC recorded, 2) Permit issued and active, 3) Builder's Risk insurance active, 4) Inspections passed in sequence, 5) Energy code compliance, 6) Wind mitigation per county, 7) Flood zone compliance, 8) Impact fees paid, 9) Utility connections approved, 10) Final inspection and CO before closing.
Permits expire after 180 days without inspection activity per Florida Statute 553.79(14). Compliance must alert when permits approach this threshold.
Florida Construction Lien Law — Notice to Owner deadlines, Contractor's Final Affidavit requirements (Fla. Stat. 713.06), and payment chain compliance.
Construction site safety compliance including fall protection, scaffolding, electrical safety, excavation standards, and personal protective equipment requirements.

daily_workflows.name: Permit Status Monitoring
steps: Review all active permits for expiration proximity (180-day rule)
Verify permit is active for every project before construction activity
critical_decisions: Whether to flag imminent permit expiration as critical

daily_workflows.name: Inspection Sequence Audit
steps: Cross-reference inspection results with county requirements
Verify inspections passed in correct sequence
Verify all required inspections completed before CO request
critical_decisions: Whether inspection sequence violation blocks next phase

daily_workflows.name: Daily Log Safety Monitoring
steps: Monitor daily logs for safety violations or code non-compliance mentions
Flag missing safety documentation (erosion control, PPE)
Route violations to Supervisao for correction

daily_workflows.name: Document Completeness Check
steps: Verify NOC filed before construction begins
Verify impact fee payments per county schedule
Check Florida Product Approval numbers on specified materials
critical_decisions: Whether missing document blocks construction progress

handoffs.trigger: Permit approaching expiration or missing documentation identified
handoffs.data_exchanged: Permit ID, days to expiration, missing document list
handoffs.impact: Expired permit = all work must stop until renewed

handoffs.trigger: Safety violation or code non-compliance detected in daily logs
handoffs.data_exchanged: Violation description, code reference, required correction
handoffs.impact: Safety violations can result in stop-work orders

handoffs.data_exchanged: Violation details, severity, affected project, recommended action
handoffs.impact: Coordenador must immediately re-route for resolution

pain_points.description: County requirements change frequently and vary significantly between Marion, Citrus, and Charlotte — difficult to maintain current knowledge
pain_points.current_workaround: Permits department maintains county contacts for updates
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated county regulation tracking and alert system

tribal_knowledge.description: Each county has different inspection requirements and sequences. Citrus requires Structural Frame + Sheathing inspections that Marion does not. Charlotte has enhanced hurricane requirements. This variation is partially documented but nuances exist.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Inspection scheduling errors; failed inspections due to wrong sequence

topics: Energy code compliance (blower door, duct leakage)
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Wind speeds per county, permit expiration rules, inspection sequences
County-specific requirements, fee schedules, special inspections
OSHA standards, incident classification, reporting requirements

compliance_requirements.requirement: All projects must have active permits before any construction activity
compliance_requirements.verification: Permit status check before each phase transition

compliance_requirements.requirement: NOC must be recorded before construction begins
compliance_requirements.verification: Document presence check in BT/Drive before first activity

compliance_requirements.requirement: Builder's Risk insurance must be active throughout construction
compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida insurance requirements for licensed contractors

compliance_requirements.requirement: CO issued before any occupancy or closing
compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida Building Code — Certificate of Occupancy
compliance_requirements.verification: CO document filed after Building Final Inspection passes

compliance_requirements.requirement: Wind mitigation features must meet county-specific wind speed requirements
compliance_requirements.verification: Structural plans review and inspection results

key_metrics.target: 100% of projects pass 10-point checklist before construction
